result:
1. Trusts client-supplied X-Forwarded-For for rate-limit keying
evidence: `rl:${req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip}` reads header directly, violates Section 2 rule 2 + 2025-09 SEV1 RCA
impact: attacker rotates XFF per request, bypasses per-IP limits, enables brute-force (same exploit path as the 2025-09 incident)
fix: key off `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; lint rule `no-direct-xff-read` should already block this
verify: add test with spoofed XFF header — limiter must key off socket remoteAddress, not header

2. Missing EXPIRE on INCR — Redis OOM risk
evidence: `await redis.incr(key)` with no paired `EXPIRE`, violates Section 2 rule 3 + 2025-11 SEV2 RCA
impact: unbounded key growth, Redis OOM eviction, edge offline (replays 2025-11 incident)
fix: wrap in `MULTI`: `incr(key)` + `expire(key, 90)`; `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint rule should flag this
verify: integration test asserts TTL > 0 after first INCR on a fresh key

3. Key missing route prefix, minute bucket, and region — collision + wrong limit semantics
evidence: key is `rl:{ip}` only; spec format is `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` with region prefix per 2025-12 RCA
impact: all routes share one counter per IP (wrong limit enforcement); no sliding window (bucket never rotates); cross-region global throttle (2025-12 replay)
fix: `rl:{region}:{trustedClientIp}:{routePrefix}:{Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)}`
verify: unit test key format per region + route; regression test from 2025-12

4. No limit check / no 429 response
evidence: code increments but never compares `count > limit` nor returns RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after`
impact: limiter is a no-op — accepts all traffic, just counts it
fix: read `[count]` from `multi.exec()`, compare against `config/rate_limits.json` lookup, return problem+json `429` body per Section 4
verify: test exceeds limit → `429` with `type:/errors/rate-limited` and `retry_after`

5. `req.ip` fallback still header-derived when Express `trust proxy` is set
evidence: `||req.ip` — Express resolves `req.ip` from XFF when `trust proxy` is enabled, so fallback is also spoofable
impact: even if XFF is absent, attacker controls `req.ip` via other forwarded headers depending on proxy config
fix: single source of truth — `trustedClientIp(req)` which validates chain against `config/trusted_proxies.json`
verify: unit test with `app.set('trust proxy', true)` + spoofed XFF → `trustedClientIp` returns socket address, not header value

raw_payload.result:
1. Trusts client-supplied X-Forwarded-For for rate-limit keying
evidence: `rl:${req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip}` reads header directly, violates Section 2 rule 2 + 2025-09 SEV1 RCA
impact: attacker rotates XFF per request, bypasses per-IP limits, enables brute-force (same exploit path as the 2025-09 incident)
fix: key off `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; lint rule `no-direct-xff-read` should already block this
verify: add test with spoofed XFF header — limiter must key off socket remoteAddress, not header

2. Missing EXPIRE on INCR — Redis OOM risk
evidence: `await redis.incr(key)` with no paired `EXPIRE`, violates Section 2 rule 3 + 2025-11 SEV2 RCA
impact: unbounded key growth, Redis OOM eviction, edge offline (replays 2025-11 incident)
fix: wrap in `MULTI`: `incr(key)` + `expire(key, 90)`; `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint rule should flag this
verify: integration test asserts TTL > 0 after first INCR on a fresh key

3. Key missing route prefix, minute bucket, and region — collision + wrong limit semantics
evidence: key is `rl:{ip}` only; spec format is `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` with region prefix per 2025-12 RCA
impact: all routes share one counter per IP (wrong limit enforcement); no sliding window (bucket never rotates); cross-region global throttle (2025-12 replay)
fix: `rl:{region}:{trustedClientIp}:{routePrefix}:{Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)}`
verify: unit test key format per region + route; regression test from 2025-12

4. No limit check / no 429 response
evidence: code increments but never compares `count > limit` nor returns RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after`
impact: limiter is a no-op — accepts all traffic, just counts it
fix: read `[count]` from `multi.exec()`, compare against `config/rate_limits.json` lookup, return problem+json `429` body per Section 4
verify: test exceeds limit → `429` with `type:/errors/rate-limited` and `retry_after`

5. `req.ip` fallback still header-derived when Express `trust proxy` is set
evidence: `||req.ip` — Express resolves `req.ip` from XFF when `trust proxy` is enabled, so fallback is also spoofable
impact: even if XFF is absent, attacker controls `req.ip` via other forwarded headers depending on proxy config
fix: single source of truth — `trustedClientIp(req)` which validates chain against `config/trusted_proxies.json`
verify: unit test with `app.set('trust proxy', true)` + spoofed XFF → `trustedClientIp` returns socket address, not header value
